reforms.description: Denmark is shifting towards project-based public research funding, establishing the High Technology Foundation with an allocation of DKK16 billion to enhance high technology research capabilities. This new funding model allows private firms to bid for funds on equal terms and ties funding to quality assessments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The shift to project-based funding is expected to enhance the quality and relevance of research, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The High Technology Foundation was established and operational by 2012.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how public research funding is allocated and managed.
reforms.source_quote: The High Technology Foundation aims to facilitate the development of high technology research and will have DKK16 billion in assets by 2012.

reforms.description: Denmark's Globalisation Strategy from 2006 aims to enhance R&D and higher education financing, promoting lifelong learning and skill development in the public sector. This includes systematic projections of future staffing and skill needs, and expanding vocational training for social and health assistants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing skills and promoting lifelong learning, the strategy supports the development of a more capable workforce, which is essential for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives outlined in the Globalisation Strategy have been put into practice since 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major initiative to align education and training with the needs of the economy, significantly impacting human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2007, a major agreement was entered between the government, the local/regional authorities and the unions covering virtually all parts of the public sector workforce.

reforms.description: Denmark has removed remaining restrictions on setting up technology transfer companies and science parks, facilitating the commercialization of research and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing restrictions on technology transfer, the reform enhances the ability of research institutions to commercialize their innovations, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The removal of restrictions has been enacted and is in effect as of 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the environment for technology transfer but does not represent a fundamental overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Remove remaining restrictions on setting up technology transfer companies and science parks.

reforms.description: The Danish government has introduced a temporary 5% tax relief for pension funds investing in the venture capital market, aimed at stimulating investment in innovative startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax relief is designed to encourage investment in startups, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax relief measure was enacted and is operational as of 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: While this reform is beneficial for the startup ecosystem, it is a temporary measure and does not represent a systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: Pension funds will have a temporary 5% tax relief for investment via entering the venture capital market.

reforms.description: The Financing Fund was created to support basic research in Denmark, managing around DKK1.5 billion in assets.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing funding for basic research, the fund supports foundational knowledge creation, which is essential for long-term innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Financing Fund has been established and is operational since 2008.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund is significant for supporting basic research but does not represent a major overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: The Financing Fund manages around DKK1.5 billion in assets that are to be used to support basic research in Denmark.
